title: Objects Launched Into Space by Country
blurb: The space age as a running tally of every satellite, probe and crewed craft each nation has put into orbit or beyond. The USSR and USA trade the lead through the Cold War, then a quiet revolution arrives: commercial mega-constellations send the United States' count vertical in the 2020s. Cumulative, so each launch adds to a country's total forever.

source.name: UNOOSA Online Index & Jonathan's Space Report
source.note: Cumulative count of objects launched into space attributed to each launching state/operator's country. Recent US totals are dominated by Starlink.

milestones.title: Sputnik opens the space age
milestones.text: The USSR launches the first artificial satellite in October 1957, and for a moment holds the entire scoreboard alone.

milestones.title: America pulls ahead
milestones.text: Fuelled by the Apollo build-up, the United States launches at a furious pace and overtakes the Soviet total in the mid-1960s.

milestones.title: The USSR retakes the lead
milestones.text: A relentless cadence of Kosmos satellites pushes the Soviet cumulative count back in front, where it stays for decades.

milestones.title: China joins the front rank
milestones.text: China's launch rate accelerates past Japan and Europe, becoming the clear third space power of the 21st century.

milestones.title: The Starlink vertical
milestones.text: Commercial mega-constellations send thousands of small satellites up at once, and the US total goes nearly vertical.
